[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] possesses the [[Scimitar/Legends|''Scimitar'']] during the series, and ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates both his acquisition of the ship and the events of ''The Phantom Menance'' to 32 BBY, thus placing the events of the first four ''Episode I Adventures'' books in 32 BBY as well.</ref>

'''''Episode I Adventures 2: The Bartokk Assassins''''' was the second installment of ''[[Star Wars]] [[Star Wars: Episode I Adventures|Episode I Adventures]]''.

{{Book-stub}}

==Publisher's summary==
''A band of Bartokk assassins is in possession of many unusual Trade Federation starfighters''.

''They have also kidnapped an innocent child.''

''The Bartokks must be stopped.''

''More than one life hangs in the balance.''

==Plot summary==
[[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] remain on the planet [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]] following their [[Episode I Adventures 1: Search for the Lost Jedi|rescue of Adi Gallia]] as they hunt for the fifty missing [[Droid starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]]. With the help of [[Boll Trinkatta]], they find his hired pilot [[Bama Vook]], who confesses that he stole the fighters and hid them so that they would not be obtained by the Trade Federation.

Unfortunately for the Jedi, they are observed and pursued by a group of Bartokk assassins working for a mysterious client. The Bartokks knock out Qui-Gon and steal all of the fighters on a frighter, using Vook's young son [[Chup-Chup]] as a hostage. During all this, [[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]] and [[Daultay Dofine/Legends|Daultay Dofine]] hunt for evidence of the fighters on Esseles, failing to notice the Jedi or the assassins.

With his master unconscious, Obi-Wan pursues in a [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]]. He shoots down a number of the fighters and boards the freighter, fighting his way through the assassins to rescue Chup-Chup. Although he retrieves half of the starfighters, he discovers that the Bartokks had already moved the other half to a different ship, now bound for [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]]. He meets up with his master and they prepare to give chase, only to discover that all signal has gone dead from the [[Jedi chapter house (Rhinnal)|Jedi chapter house]] on [[Rhinnal/Legends|Rhinnal]], where Adi Gallia had been taken. They must choose whether to pursue the fighters or check in on their colleague.

Elsewhere, [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] watches in displeasure as the Jedi come close to stumbling on elements of his plan. Since the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidians]] have failed him, he turns to his most trusted agent, [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], to retrieve the missing fighters.
